name: Hands Off Our Healthcare (via Working Families Party)
subject: Less than twelve days to save Medicaid
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Working Families Party PAC (77 Sands St
body:
| | | Peter, The GOP’s plot to take health care and food away from millions of working families — and put the extra money into billionaire’s pockets — is entering its final phase. This week, House committees began making their final edits and adjustments, with an ultimate goal of pushing the budget through the House before Memorial Day.1 Those edits are now officially on paper, and they are disastrous. According to research gathered from the Congressional Budget Office, **13 million people would lose their health care coverage under this Republican budget.** 2 **_Thirteen million._** [We only have a few days before the full Congress is set to vote on these cuts. Join an emergency Mass Call TOMORROW, May 15th at 8pm ET / 5pm PT, to hear how you can help ramp up pressure on Republicans and prevent them from stealing our health care. RSVP here:](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMbGj-ca0TYSsYMZBvIweYdJxZAP5xLg1YZaKIinIFmIxwYs7z7uDuwZqo8C4IDqBtX1N11lpxYC-RdVXoMQFlDO0mQhjkDOdLjizcsIWawrNUPz8gzSMYHJaG0an2tT8QRxrG9FsuxQuEIJb_0WA52QQLxFpdgLnWm4fvMp42TbSbvxoVDrxAW0Djl04x19r8dqOD8A9GSjYJ71i4aP2pUPLWnNWdg28Cw5usIFtKdZrEDWvUMtCbVGT_sTEw3NrcEtTUzu6ou72iYfoAy5cml8/4gh/CDFdEArRS42IA9xHoFNUsg/h0/h001.HDN_PbK7pNtaWNmOIBQejd6fg2CyK-23QP1Kd4hwE5I) **[Join our rapid response Mass Call](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMbGj-ca0TYSsYMZBvIweYdJxZAP5xLg1YZaKIinIFmIxwYs7z7uDuwZqo8C4IDqBtX1N11lpxYC-RdVXoMQFlDO0mQhjkDOdLjizcsIWawrNMw162sWr78g9JaEmXCHsHa99xYDSCWE0Pj1lfa2JjWLCoienOFPJlTw88TsIQE5Ia-R2ilkK0hkHZ8r9meq5upp5PyE-E7ObQqfrDPob0BYWIP0fl8Uh6u-RyAYA77hRqf1GvUlaG0tIGfgdfASV1Bjn4LV3WGKtTgyFJgiSRw8/4gh/CDFdEArRS42IA9xHoFNUsg/h1/h001.reO0a4wj5AUAoAWXENZg_q4LENf5zJvNke_MsbohHkA)** If this budget passes, countless rural hospitals that rely on Medicaid funding to stay open will likely be forced to shut down or reduce their services.3 And an enormous strain will be put on state budgets, forcing them to reduce or eliminate other programs that families need too.4 **This budget would be an unmitigated disaster for the actual people in our country, all because a bunch of billionaires want Trump to subsidize their yacht collection.** Moments like these demand action from all of us, Peter. We’ve already been hitting the streets and the phone lines non-stop since Trump took office. Millions showed up to the national _Hands Off!_ protests. Hundreds of thousands marched on May Day. And we’ve been showing up at town halls, Congressional offices, and phonebanks virtually every day. **But we all need to redouble our efforts between now and Memorial Day.** Families are _already_ being forced to stretch every dollar in response to Trump’s pillaging. Now they’re on the verge of throwing even more of us into debt, all to give their donors an even larger slice of the pie. [We have to stop them. RSVP for Thursday’s mass call and join health care professionals, labor leaders, organizers, and working people from across the country to learn more about how you can help save Medicaid in the coming days.](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMbGj-ca0TYSsYMZBvIweYdJxZAP5xLg1YZaKIinIFmIxwYs7z7uDuwZqo8C4IDqBtX1N11lpxYC-RdVXoMQFlDO0mQhjkDOdLjizcsIWawrNi8b9N4lxMLZSQLh4iO0CvVVNTXB-RcE9ftgIM_s-z42uGwcL6-lNzwf6nXFrg-5sjKUguj-EPhZ5zt6yy4_UTBALZUClk2oNDAun1GpL1-aAEgdK784tD1LocBvb0NYTctkwN8EFrWimRvdSgp9hs5tP6cp4JLa4JLA9AsVdNLk/4gh/CDFdEArRS42IA9xHoFNUsg/h2/h001.GULgpmZjJ5z6AyZRpQ_3C2hsC1g28E6PBIB5pNCvzfg) More from us soon, Team WFP Sources: 1\. clean_body: | | | Peter, The GOP’s plot to take health care and food away from millions of working families — and put the extra money into billionaire’s pockets — is entering its final phase. This week, House committees began making their final edits and adjustments, with an ultimate goal of pushing the budget through the House before Memorial Day.1 Those edits are now officially on paper, and they are disastrous. According to research gathered from the Congressional Budget Office, 13 million people would lose their health care coverage under this Republican budget. 2 Thirteen million. If this budget passes, countless rural hospitals that rely on Medicaid funding to stay open will likely be forced to shut down or reduce their services.3 And an enormous strain will be put on state budgets, forcing them to reduce or eliminate other programs that families need too.4 This budget would be an unmitigated disaster for the actual people in our country, all because a bunch of billionaires want Trump to subsidize their yacht collection. Moments like these demand action from all of us, Peter. We’ve already been hitting the streets and the phone lines non-stop since Trump took office. Millions showed up to the national Hands Off! protests. Hundreds of thousands marched on May Day. And we’ve been showing up at town halls, Congressional offices, and phonebanks virtually every day. But we all need to redouble our efforts between now and Memorial Day. Families are already being forced to stretch every dollar in response to Trump’s pillaging. Now they’re on the verge of throwing even more of us into debt, all to give their donors an even larger slice of the pie. More from us soon, Team WFP Sources: 1\. , NPR, May 13, 2025 2\. , U.S. Committee on Energy and Commerce, May 11, 2025 3\. , KFF, February 23, 2023 4\. , The Hilltop Institute, May 6, 2025 Thoughts? Feedback? We want to hear it. Reply directly to this email and let us know. This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. ## Baseball Discovers Life After Death
Greetings from the sports desk located somewhere below the main deck of the Good Pirate Ship RedState. Sammy the Shark and Karl the Kraken are taking advantage of the spring weather to get in some much needed exercise ... Those fish crackers aren’t going to work themselves off. name: The People's Promise [via Voter Action Project]
subject: We need you to sign on
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
We were promised a government for and by the people. But that's not what we're getting. This administration has made one thing clear: they don’t serve us. They serve themselves — and the billionaires who helped put them in power.
That's why we must build a better future together! Join us and add your name to show that we will not be governed by greed, chaos, or a handful of unelected billionaires >> [https://peoplespromise.us/add-your-name-2/?source=043025swap-ifvap&referrer=group-integrity-first-voter-action-project&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.everyaction.com%2F9bivrSvjFEim0QMMvgcQrA2]
SIGN HERE NOW ⇨ [https://peoplespromise.us/add-your-name-2/?source=043025swap-ifvap&referrer=group-integrity-first-voter-action-project&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.everyaction.com%2F9bivrSvjFEim0QMMvgcQrA2]
Voter Action Project [https://secure.everyaction.com/Qyv_-_BiF0iduOUt5sZ0yw2?am=25.00&contactdata=tQggVzYnS/s0a/zs+A8jwGfzYgvGV8krG59eVQPrU94izuGfKQHlIM/toyQ+tkoz74KaL+fr6HlbpoqxN61Tsiw/LnoTCOEruoSn68dvp/Y=]
Folks,
President Trump and his allies have spent the past 100 days attacking our rights, undermining our democracy, and enriching the ultra-wealthy, while driving up the cost of living for working-class Americans. It’s a deliberate strategy to distract and divide us while they seize power and wealth.
We see through their games. It’s time to join together and demand something different — not just by resisting their agenda, but by offering our own that guarantees justice, equality, and opportunity for ALL.
That’s why we’re calling on every elected official—from Congress to city hall—to deliver on what we’re calling The People’s Promise .
Learn more and sign on to The People's Promise today >> [https://peoplespromise.us/add-your-name-2/?source=043025swap-ifvap&referrer=group-integrity-first-voter-action-project&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.everyaction.com%2F9bivrSvjFEim0QMMvgcQrA2]
SIGN HERE NOW ⇨ [https://peoplespromise.us/add-your-name-2/?source=043025swap-ifvap&referrer=group-integrity-first-voter-action-project&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.everyaction.com%2F9bivrSvjFEim0QMMvgcQrA2]
Here are the key points:
* An Economy That Works for Everyone: All workers earn a livable wage and have the right to unionize. Americans can afford essentials like quality healthcare, housing, and childcare.
* A Government for the People: The wealthiest among us pay their fair share in taxes. Every child can count on the quality education they deserve, and every person has access to a strong safety net they can count on when they need it.
* Equal Rights & Opportunity for All: Nobody’s freedom can be taken without due process. Our votes are protected and counted. And our neighborhoods are free from hate and discrimination.
These aren’t radical demands—they’re the bare minimum. It’s time for our elected leaders to deliver.
Add your name right now and demand a return of government for the people and by the people immediately >> [https://peoplespromise.us/add-your-name-2/?source=043025swap-ifvap&referrer=group-integrity-first-voter-action-project&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.everyaction.com%2F9bivrSvjFEim0QMMvgcQrA2]
SIGN HERE NOW ⇨ [https://peoplespromise.us/add-your-name-2/?source=043025swap-ifvap&referrer=group-integrity-first-voter-action-project&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.everyaction.com%2F9bivrSvjFEim0QMMvgcQrA2]
https://secure.everyaction.com/Qyv_-_BiF0iduOUt5sZ0yw2?am=20&amtOpts=&recurringAm=20&recurringAmtOpts=&is_optimized_ask=true&contactdata=tQggVzYnS/s0a/zs+A8jwGfzYgvGV8krG59eVQPrU94izuGfKQHlIM/toyQ+tkoz74KaL+fr6HlbpoqxN61Tsiw/LnoTCOEruoSn68dvp/Y= [https://secure.everyaction.com/Qyv_-_BiF0iduOUt5sZ0yw2?am=20&amtOpts=&recurringAm=20&recurringAmtOpts=&is_optimized_ask=true&contactdata=tQggVzYnS/s0a/zs+A8jwGfzYgvGV8krG59eVQPrU94izuGfKQHlIM/toyQ+tkoz74KaL+fr6HlbpoqxN61Tsiw/LnoTCOEruoSn68dvp/Y=]
https://www.facebook.com/voteraction [https://www.facebook.com/voteraction] https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org [https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org] https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/ [https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/] https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/ [https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/]
Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.
Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [https://secure.everyaction.com/Qyv_-_BiF0iduOUt5sZ0yw2]
Donate Now [https://secure.everyaction.com/Qyv_-_BiF0iduOUt5sZ0yw2?am=20&amtOpts=&recurringAm=20&recurringAmtOpts=&is_optimized_ask=true&contactdata=tQggVzYnS/s0a/zs+A8jwGfzYgvGV8krG59eVQPrU94izuGfKQHlIM/toyQ+tkoz74KaL+fr6HlbpoqxN61Tsiw/LnoTCOEruoSn68dvp/Y=]

name: Deb Haaland
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Mark Kelly for Senate
body:
I’ll get right to the point.
#

Hey everybody, it’s Mark Kelly. My friend Deb Haaland is running for Governor in New Mexico and needs our help. I hope you’ll read the message below from her, and [**rush a split donation between Deb's campaign and mine.**](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1389?c0=ms-dh-em-mark-kelly.UA6FA9&refcode=em250514-1-90&t=1&akid=6068%2E6688874%2EyBrK3E)
---
Hi Friend, it’s Deb.
I’ll get right to the point: [**Today I’m asking you to make a split $3 contribution to support my campaign for Governor of New Mexico and Mark Kelly.](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1389?c0=ms-dh-em-mark-kelly.UA6FA9&refcode=em250514-1-90&t=2&akid=6068%2E6688874%2EyBrK3E)** And if you give me just a moment, I’ll explain why.
With our May mid-month fundraising deadline coming up quickly, here’s why your grassroots contribution is so critical:
* Your $3 can help us reach our goal of $15,000 before May 15.

* Your $3 will send a fierce message to our Republican opponent and Donald Trump’s allies in New Mexico, who are trying to flip our state red — that the momentum is with our campaign.

* Your $0.00 will help us show the strength of this grassroots movement, and prove to everyone that we’re ready to win and take on the big fights for families across New Mexico.

**We still need to bring in a lot more donations between now and our big deadline at midnight on May 15. [So if you can, please rush a split donation of $3 right now and help us keep our momentum going.**](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1389?c0=ms-dh-em-mark-kelly.UA6FA9&refcode=em250514-1-90&t=3&akid=6068%2E6688874%2EyBrK3E)
**[DONATE NOW](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1389?c0=ms-dh-em-mark-kelly.UA6FA9&refcode=em250514-1-90&t=4&akid=6068%2E6688874%2EyBrK3E)**
Thank you again for everything you do,
Deb

Paid For By Mark Kelly for Senate
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a very important way we communicate with supporters like you, but if you want to, you can [ unsubscribe here](https://act.markkelly.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe?t=5&akid=6068%2E6688874%2EyBrK3E).
Mark Kelly was a Captain in the U.S. Navy and astronaut. Use of his military rank, job titles and photographs in uniform does not imply endorsement by the Department of the Navy or Department of Defense and reference to NASA does not imply endorsement by the National Aeronautics and Space Administration.

name: GOTV PAC
subject: The old guard failed us. It’s time for new blood.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY GET OUT THE VOTE PAC NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE OR ANY CANDIDATE'S COMMITTEE
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?refcode=Email3182025&amount=25 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?refcode=Email3182025&amount=25]
POLITICO:
“Just 40 percent of Democrats approve of the job performance of congressional Democrats , compared to 49 percent who disapprove .”
Peter,
We need to stop pretending everything's fine within the Democratic Party.
We can’t keep talking about Trump's falling approval rating — while not acknowledging our own.
So let's get honest: The Democratic establishment helped create this moment, and too many of our leaders are still asleep at the wheel while our country creeps closer and closer to becoming a full-blown oligarchy.
Voters aren't interested in version 1.2 of a broken system!
They want fresh leaders and candidates with the energy to fight, shake things up, and put people first, and that's exactly who GOTV PAC is working to elect.
We're not here to polish up the same losing consultants and tired career politicians. We're investing in new blood from everyday people stepping up to lead the Democratic Party in the most competitive districts across the country. Because the old guard won't fix what they still refuse to acknowledge is broken!
If you're done watching Democrats sleepwalk into a Trump dictatorship, please donate $20 or more right now to help us bring new energy and leadership to the front lines of this fight. We're not just investing in races where we can beat MAGA candidates; we're investing in the leaders ready to take this fight directly to Trump. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?refcode=Email05142025&amount=25]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:

The truth is hard, but it's also freeing. We don't have to settle for this.
GOTV PAC

Get Out the Vote PAC mobilizes voters often overlooked by mainstream campaigns to help Democratic candidates win in the most competitive districts.
Our professional phone bankers specialize in long- and short-term GOTV campaigns, event registration, voting assistance in heavily gerrymandered states, fundraising, and call time management. Our mission is to flip red districts blue, any elected position from dog walker to Congress member.
Learn more at: gotv.vote [https://www.gotv.vote/]

Our mission is to Flip the House by supporting targeted Congressional districts and down-ballot races within those districts. | | Team, Our democracy is under attack from the far right. At the core of this assault? **Project 2025** , the playbook for MAGA extremism. As we suspected, just four months into Donald Trump’s presidency, he’s working to bring Project 2025 to fruition — _meaning that the freedom to vote is at risk, along with our democracy. _ Take a look at these actions and how they connect to Project 2025: → Buried in Trump’s order to pause federal funds earlier this year was a deliberate attack on election security. The freeze targeted $55 million in Election Security Grants — grants administered by the U.S. Election Assistance Commission that are essential for upholding election security and modernizing voter infrastructure. → Project 2025 has a goal of undermining trust in the electoral process and weakening our elections’ cybersecurity protections. The plan even states that the president could withhold funds to consolidate power and overcome the bureaucracy — ultimately to advance the MAGA agenda. → With cabinet picks like **Pam Bondi for U.S. Attorney General and Harmeet Dhillon to lead the Department of Justice’s Civil Rights division,** Trump has given authority to two of his cronies that aided attempts to overturn the 2020 election. Less independence at the Justice Department can mean fewer checks on the MAGA agenda of election interference and voter suppression. → Project 2025’s proposal to dismantle the Justice Department’s Civil Rights Division would severely weaken enforcement of the Voting Rights Act, threatening access to the ballot for Black and brown communities and undermining decades of voting protections. → **One of Trump’s recent executive orders** — a blatant, illegal attempt to seize control over elections — calls for prosecuting state and local election officials if they don’t go along with his anti-voter agenda and his power grab. → Project 2025 lays out how to prosecute state and local elections officials by twisting a law originally used against the KKK’s violent voter suppression. Under this playbook, the Trump regime can enforce its own vision for how to run elections. [**Fair Fight is not backing down from the threats of Project 2025, and we’ll keep exposing and pushing back against the far right’s anti-voter agenda. clean_body: An update on Project 2025 and how we’re fighting back. | | | Team, Our democracy is under attack from the far right. At the core of this assault? Project 2025 , the playbook for MAGA extremism. As we suspected, just four months into Donald Trump’s presidency, he’s working to bring Project 2025 to fruition — meaning that the freedom to vote is at risk, along with our democracy. Take a look at these actions and how they connect to Project 2025: → Buried in Trump’s order to pause federal funds earlier this year was a deliberate attack on election security. The freeze targeted $55 million in Election Security Grants — grants administered by the U.S. Election Assistance Commission that are essential for upholding election security and modernizing voter infrastructure. → Project 2025 has a goal of undermining trust in the electoral process and weakening our elections’ cybersecurity protections. The plan even states that the president could withhold funds to consolidate power and overcome the bureaucracy — ultimately to advance the MAGA agenda. → With cabinet picks like Pam Bondi for U.S. Attorney General and Harmeet Dhillon to lead the Department of Justice’s Civil Rights division, Trump has given authority to two of his cronies that aided attempts to overturn the 2020 election. Less independence at the Justice Department can mean fewer checks on the MAGA agenda of election interference and voter suppression. → Project 2025’s proposal to dismantle the Justice Department’s Civil Rights Division would severely weaken enforcement of the Voting Rights Act, threatening access to the ballot for Black and brown communities and undermining decades of voting protections. → One of Trump’s recent executive orders — a blatant, illegal attempt to seize control over elections — calls for prosecuting state and local election officials if they don’t go along with his anti-voter agenda and his power grab. → Project 2025 lays out how to prosecute state and local elections officials by twisting a law originally used against the KKK’s violent voter suppression. Under this playbook, the Trump regime can enforce its own vision for how to run elections. = | = Clearly, the far-right is targeting elections because they’re scared of democratic accountability. They know that when we the people have the freedom to take our seat at the table and our place at the ballot box, we can elect leaders who govern in our interests, ensure every voice is heard, and make the promise of democracy real for us all. Here’s what we can expect: Extremist operatives will continue to lay the groundwork for mass voter purges, dismantling election administration, and disrupting election certification processes. Here’s what not to expect: Fair Fight backing down from this fight. As a national leader in the fight for voting rights and democracy, our place in this fight is on the frontlines. = If you've saved payment info with ActBlue Express, your donation will process automatically: = | = ---|--- = | = ---|--- = | = ---|--- In solidarity, The Fair Fight Team = | * 1270 Caroline Street | Suite D120-430 | Atlanta, GA 30307 ©2025 Fair Fight - All rights reserved. body:
We’ve tried to make this campaign as different as possible

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107703259/545549990/-240735101?refcode=email-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NTc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmN2QwYTBlZS0wOTMxLWYwMTEtOGIzZC02MDQ1YmRlZDhjY2EiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=obnsOuOKXgSl0GDmqlbNJp6PegdihGrgItA6xZjbxyM=) Hey there, If you’re burned out from candidates asking for your support, or you just need to know more before you’re ready to personally invest in a campaign, we get that. We really do. (We aren’t immune from the flood of emails and texts from campaigns either!) That’s why we’ve tried to make these messages as different as possible. No fake 5X matches. No “We’re DOOMED unless you donate!!!” subject lines either. This campaign is about laying out the stakes and giving it to you straight, not scaring you into donating. But that doesn’t mean we’re going to hide the truth from you, even if it’s not always what you want to hear. And the truth is, Sharice needs your support more than ever. Listen, Sharice will never be one of those politicians who is too afraid to speak up. Even in a competitive district like KS-03, she’s going to call out Donald Trump and National Republicans every single time they try to hurt Kansas families — because that’s the kind of leadership our state needs right now. But that leadership also makes her a target. Over the years, National Republicans have launched attack after attack at Sharice — and now, they believe next year’s midterms is the year they can finally replace her. [_**So can Sharice count on your support right here, right now? Her leadership truly stands out in Washington, but the fact is, it’s going to require grassroots support to keep her there.**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107703260/545549991/-945052836?refcode=email_051425_MMFR2&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500,1000&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NTc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmN2QwYTBlZS0wOTMxLWYwMTEtOGIzZC02MDQ1YmRlZDhjY2EiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=obnsOuOKXgSl0GDmqlbNJp6PegdihGrgItA6xZjbxyM=) _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | | | [Donate $5 →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107703261/545549992/-945052836?refcode=email_051425_MMFR2&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500,1000&amount=5&express_lane=true&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NTc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmN2QwYTBlZS0wOTMxLWYwMTEtOGIzZC02MDQ1YmRlZDhjY2EiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=obnsOuOKXgSl0GDmqlbNJp6PegdihGrgItA6xZjbxyM=)

Shawnee, KS 66216
United States
clean_body: We’ve tried to make this campaign as different as possible = Hey there, If you’re burned out from candidates asking for your support, or you just need to know more before you’re ready to personally invest in a campaign, we get that. We really do. (We aren’t immune from the flood of emails and texts from campaigns either!) That’s why we’ve tried to make these messages as different as possible. No fake 5X matches. No “We’re DOOMED unless you donate!!!” subject lines either. This campaign is about laying out the stakes and giving it to you straight, not scaring you into donating. But that doesn’t mean we’re going to hide the truth from you, even if it’s not always what you want to hear. And the truth is, Sharice needs your support more than ever. Listen, Sharice will never be one of those politicians who is too afraid to speak up. Even in a competitive district like KS-03, she’s going to call out Donald Trump and National Republicans every single time they try to hurt Kansas families — because that’s the kind of leadership our state needs right now. But that leadership also makes her a target. Over the years, National Republicans have launched attack after attack at Sharice — and now, they believe next year’s midterms is the year they can finally replace her. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | | = | = | | = | = | | = | = Thank you so much, Sharice for Congress HQ | This email was sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a key way for us to stay in touch and make sure you get the latest updates from our campaign. But if you would like to . | | = | PAID FOR BY SHARICE FOR CONGRESS Sharice for Congress 13851 W. 63rd St. #303 Shawnee, KS 66216 United States

From slashing Park Service budgets to green-lighting the sale of protected lands, this is just the next round in his assault on the places that define who we are as a nation.
Let’s be clear: Trump is doing this to line the pockets of himself and his billionaire friends, putting profits over people and greed over generations of conservation.
That’s why Gabe’s leadership in launching the bipartisan Public Lands Caucus is so crucial right now. Gabe is willing to work across the aisle to make real progress, because protecting our public lands isn’t a partisan issue — it’s an American one.
We need more leaders like Gabe who will fight back and stand up for our parks, our history and our future — no matter who’s in the White House. That’s why Gabe’s leadership in launching the bipartisan Public Lands Caucus is so crucial right now. Gabe is willing to work across the aisle to make real progress, because protecting our public lands isn’t a partisan issue — it’s an American one. We need more leaders like Gabe who will fight back and stand up for our parks, our history and our future — no matter who’s in the White House. = If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process now. | = | = | = | = | = | = In this fight together, Team Vasquez | | | ---|---|--- = Together, we flipped NM-02 in 2022. Now, Gabe is busy in Congress fighting for reproductive choice, economic opportunity, affordable healthcare, and the best interests of the people. Gabe won the most competitive Democratic seat in Congress in 2022, and is running to be a champion for working families and build an economy that benefits everyone.** | = = Paid for by Gabe Vasquez for Congress Gabe Vasquez for Congress Drawer L Mesilla, NM 88046 United States If you would like to unsubscribe, click here: To receive less email from Gabe Vasquez for Congress, . You can also =.

Judicial Watch made history last month when new developments in
landmark legal actions led to the inactivation, processing, or removal
of more than five million ineligible names from voter rolls
nationwide. “Judicial Watch’s clean-up of over five million dirty
names from voter rolls is a historic achievement for clean
elections,” said JW President Tom Fitton. “I have no doubt that
Judicial Watch’s election integrity heavy lifting helped stop the
steal in 2024. But there are millions of more names to be removed from
voter rolls.”

American-made films have captured the world’s imagination for over a century. They are one of our greatest cultural exports.
As a film and television producer for over two decades, Laura understands firsthand that to keep making films here at home – and support millions of American jobs in the process – we need real solutions like a national film tax credit, not cost-increasing tariffs.
That’s why Laura is working on the federal level to bring incentives so that production stays in America, allowing us to continue sharing our stories of who we are to the world.
[**Watch Laura’s interview here >> **](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107703846/545551699/-1698420193?refcode=em-5.14.25-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDM1MzYiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNjQzOWQwMGItMGMzMS1mMDExLThiM2QtNjA0NWJkZWQ4Y2NhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=KuMG2ki9KIH9SXd0O9K-6-Vl9eZUbs--4dZThgc5k2o=)
Thank you,
Laura Friedman HQ

clean_body: | = Friend, Laura joined CNBC to make clear how Trump’s plan to tariff overseas film shoots isn’t the answer to runaway production. But her plan for real support is. = = | **= American-made films have captured the world’s imagination for over a century. They are one of our greatest cultural exports. As a film and television producer for over two decades, Laura understands firsthand that to keep making films here at home – and support millions of American jobs in the process – we need real solutions like a national film tax credit, not cost-increasing tariffs. That’s why Laura is working on the federal level to bring incentives so that production stays in America, allowing us to continue sharing our stories of who we are to the world. = Thank you, Laura Friedman HQ = =** | | Paid for by Laura Friedman for Congress Laura's campaign doesn't take a dime from corporate PACs and completely relies on grassroots supporters like you! body:
Together, we’re fighting side-by-side against Republicans’ cruel Medicaid cuts. | | | [](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.X6XUC7u8ppFLNtMO_55SZlNseb7gEYIOhm_EWnhzEOZKmTtnV3k2h4V2x_O3H7rdW2az5rgktBwzxQ8Ml9Wgkmv85qW-UsBonL4KRa0bcNqdICU6T3NBDdNIZG7w7wEWagAKMZbD6yK5h_eZEQ_vIzNNA4QzBuKt8uTlnPeDnpkFlG8_79PjeLszRn0fEFlQ3rrM_A3uPB0qspSdhuHfWUFMbeEfz7a-NfGtWGB9Bk41AVFld0wdC30_AK2bEjhbBRitsMK3MLb_7YdThiwLwB2bv7BYsseDAXgXyFvZXM6-0QxETTaRrOWchlVozyCah04y1VX3N-UaXNrOv1baocrXeRW7yL0bskRyBHYWBTaWkfmJ2KZ7VuPYpQatRign/4gh/z8KmNKopTLCedt6Cv1U9MQ/h0/h001.3YfVkfv7L5Rf6H3dl_21E38hWKGb_qQH3lHItNaib_Q)
---
Friend, Congressional Republicans are finalizing their budget that would kick tens of millions of people off of Medicaid and SNAP―cruelty that is meant for one purpose: to pay for trillions of dollars in tax cuts for billionaires like Donald Trump and Elon Musk. We’re fighting back! **We can block the largest-ever cuts to these critical, lifesaving programs by passing the Hands Off Medicaid and SNAP discharge petition.** I added my name last week as an original co-sponsor. We need 218 signatures from U.S. Representatives in order to force a vote in the U.S. House―and right now we’re at 205! [**Add your name as a grassroots co-sponsor of the Hands Off Medicaid and SNAP discharge petition to urge both Democrats and Republicans to sign-on today, before next week’s budget vote.**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm39uDKkapQVnT18L0ER7M9Fm2Mas4UdC2klm0c-ARXo9KZqkD2XBimLszUXQWgUjZV-BjO6uc4cI2YUwUIezh9r5asdNRw2hQ9kHwoNF-P4kuMPME4V0i-7iA9m4ogPEkadaPs0UdR4acZDCtQ6aSGCCfhbXe8Zs-w8S6RDVFwRmXOCVBWKYsrBeGQq6DWdRMwes10RKLMXCno6ZqTDZDOtzntXRG1rUXz13aIkG_zn8JScy_ELjLVEpB_djcBwSQDqrMPlQcMcZjGInwik6rhSfhWBoiX0VV3BvJir6hBkZ9ODdTJzYlQ-LPD9EsVn8UshCHnrW71wvhffDGvS4jMviNitUUpc1h1N37jinGU1E7iuyBZ0BgZXy2sItay_ROQjqBU1PlMR5LdI7JZfuR6Suv7SdwfQDgLVMZHhExwMvHJ82JWVutfe4HwYTG7vaDVyEluJovw6vdilUoEuQ8VQo/4gh/z8KmNKopTLCedt6Cv1U9MQ/h1/h001.QZEr9RakqMqmB9zbqbG7iIDuGmjihRT1wVbstGLXTaU) | [ADD YOUR NAME](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm39uDKkapQVnT18L0ER7M9Fm2Mas4UdC2klm0c-ARXo9KZqkD2XBimLszUXQWgUjZV-BjO6uc4cI2YUwUIezh9r5asdNRw2hQ9kHwoNF-P4kuMPME4V0i-7iA9m4ogPEkadQEJ26Cok8OTKplUJxxIusbKppoCDkO2OSCf943_J5m60FhdPYr9tiF1LBd0_sRtL_8dY6yxpV5r6RyJvuNpPfMp1TpLKuUubzgft171cdwRILdVyHSABsQwvG4Y2lc3xV2s7YVb6xyTQdLG56UkR0odS-8Cjmov_84CKqhbgqgyG7SxW6QNgUkriK6lCjlzEp3_goYq8ZWio3QKe32MCCS5d1WrGXyJvG4daISvuFUtC_UJV3zhCndrQiK3n0ED6Gr-E5IIAS6D_NYkDuuodojU4k7-pKApXcjtzq_GyI16noJGclq3OzKR3rEhec9tXD8-onvqGQFVLwvJQkdjZE/4gh/z8KmNKopTLCedt6Cv1U9MQ/h2/h001.oGx96hAbGGTbh0CZBof-ix-5td0zKWZrUI-wt_IuNoY)
---
A new report from the Michigan Department of Health & Human Services states that up to 750,000 Michiganders could lose access to lifesaving healthcare. But these proposed cuts will impact us all.
Cuts to Medicaid will result in closures of rural hospitals, drug treatment programs, nursing homes, and reduce access to mental health services―making it harder for everyone to access the healthcare they need.
More than 40% of births in the U.S. are covered by Medicaid, including roughly 50% of births in rural areas. Medicaid also covers more than 60% of nursing home care.
**Together, we’re fighting side-by-side against Republicans’ cruel Medicaid cuts that would devastate families, seniors, and people with disabilities.**
[**For many of our neighbors, Medicaid is life or death. clean_body: Together, we’re fighting side-by-side against Republicans’ cruel Medicaid cuts. | | | Friend, Congressional Republicans are finalizing their budget that would kick tens of millions of people off of Medicaid and SNAP―cruelty that is meant for one purpose: to pay for trillions of dollars in tax cuts for billionaires like Donald Trump and Elon Musk. We’re fighting back! We can block the largest-ever cuts to these critical, lifesaving programs by passing the Hands Off Medicaid and SNAP discharge petition. I added my name last week as an original co-sponsor. We need 218 signatures from U.S. Representatives in order to force a vote in the U.S. House―and right now we’re at 205! | A new report from the Michigan Department of Health & Human Services states that up to 750,000 Michiganders could lose access to lifesaving healthcare. But these proposed cuts will impact us all. Cuts to Medicaid will result in closures of rural hospitals, drug treatment programs, nursing homes, and reduce access to mental health services―making it harder for everyone to access the healthcare they need. More than 40% of births in the U.S. are covered by Medicaid, including roughly 50% of births in rural areas. Medicaid also covers more than 60% of nursing home care. Together, we’re fighting side-by-side against Republicans’ cruel Medicaid cuts that would devastate families, seniors, and people with disabilities. While Elon Musk, Donald Trump, and congressional Republicans seek to destroy programs and services relied upon by tens of millions of people―just to pay for more tax cuts for the wealthy―we’re demanding a government and an economy that works for everyone, not just the wealthy few. Always serving you, Rashida Rashida Tlaib for Congress PO Box 32777 Detroit, MI 48232 | Paid for by Rashida Tlaib for Congress | | Sent via . _**Official Democracy on the Line Petition**_

**Petition for:** Peter Willis

**27 Cresco signatures needed**
Peter, this is outrageous.

Trump is planning a massive military parade for June 14 – HIS BIRTHDAY – and wants to blow **$92 MILLION** of our tax dollars on it.

❌Not to honor veterans.
❌Not to support active-duty troops.
❌Not to bolster national defense.

_Just to celebrate himself._

While working families are struggling with rising costs, Trump is planning a self-glorifying spectacle, and he expects the American people to foot the bill.

**That’s why Democracy on the Line is organizing a national petition to stop this taxpayer-funded ego trip before it starts.**

**[We need 27 more Cresco signatures before midnight to show overwhelming public opposition: »](https://click.everyaction.com/k/107703979/545552122/1433943236?refcode=DOTL_20250514_EMPET_E2_T0_AC&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNS8xLzk0MjI1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImI3ZGUxZWIyLTBjMzEtZjAxMS04YjNkLTYwNDViZGVkOGNjYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=nEx-SiJk6XrVlT5Xfp-PHWjq0s1c_t1gStrXyiZVGAE=&emci=65316658-f82c-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&emdi=b7de1eb2-0c31-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&ceid=14887570)**

name: John James
subject: Michigan can’t afford for us to fall short
disclaimer_text: Paid for by John James for MI, 35744 Van Dyke Avenue, Sterling Heights, MI 48312
body:
| | | Patriot, We’re halfway through May—and we’re falling dangerously behind. **Michigan is at a crossroads. Under Democrats, crime is up, families and businesses are being crushed by overregulation, and our values are under siege by radicals in Lansing.** I’m running for my family. I’m running for your family. I’m running to _save Michigan_. Hey Derek, Team Fetterman here with an important update: We’ve got a huge mid-month fundraising deadline coming up Thursday at 11:59 p.m., and we *need* to hit our $20,000 goal.
Before we ask you to [**toss in a few bucks**](https://act.johnfetterman.com/go/51110?t=1&akid=10093%2E434096%2E_6qV_6) to help us get there, here are two things to know:
**1\. Republicans are trying to cut Social Security + Medicaid so they can hand out *more* tax breaks to rich guys.** Seriously, they wanna gut programs millions of working families rely on.
**2\. We need your help to fight back.** Grassroots donors like you are the reason we’re able to keep fighting for regular folks. Every dollar gets us closer to hitting our $20,000 goal and shows the GOP we won’t back down.
[**_So if you're able to chip in $5 or whatever you can afford before the deadline, we’d be really grateful._**](https://act.johnfetterman.com/go/51111?t=2&akid=10093%2E434096%2E_6qV_6)👇

name: INDEMS
subject: The GOP’s never ending cycle of corruption and incompetence
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Indiana Democratic Party, www
body:
|
---
Peter, Just yesterday, reporting from NOTUS showed that new Sixth District Republican U.S. Rep. Jefferson Shreve was caught making millions of dollars worth of stock trades in his first four months in office. On top of it, the IEDC sold off two properties in Boone County for thousands of dollars less than they bought them for just years ago. This incompetence is costing Hoosier taxpayers. **It’s clear. Republicans in Indiana don’t care just how blatant their corruption and incompetence is. Twenty-plus years of one party control means they think they can get away with this on the backs of Hoosier families.** This is just weeks after Micah Beckwith and Diego Morales were caught using taxpayer funds to buy luxury vehicles. Hoosiers — we can do better. My situation is in your hands, Fellow American. I'm writing today to give you an update and to make a humble plea. _Look - this is from the bottom of my heart, so _please_ read this message to the end._ My team and I put together a historic win in 2021 to help flip all three of Virginia's highest offices red. Ever since becoming Lt. Governor, I've been logging big wins for my friends, neighbors, and every fellow Virginian. Now, I'm running for Governor so we can continue to keep up the amazing work we've been doing here in the Commonwealth and make history again. **But right now I have a few _SERIOUS_ challenges:**
---
**I was _legally_ barred from accepting even a dime in support of my campaign while I was in the Virginia legislative session.** But my Democrat opponent, Abigail Spanberger, was fundraising _**BIG TIME**_ while I was working hard in our capital. **Seriously** _**, a far-Left group injected $500K into Abigail Spanberger 's campaign coffers and there wasn't a thing I could do in response!**_
---
Now Spanberger has an _ENORMOUS_ fundraising advantage, Fellow American. Polls show this race is tied, but Spanberger has a serious cash advantage._If we don 't make up the difference, our conservative mission _will_ be cut short. _ **Today, I 'm making a plea to you as a Christian, a mother, and a Marine; _please send in any amount you can spare to support my campaign!_**

clean_body: | | | My situation is in your hands, Fellow American. I'm writing today to give you an update and to make a humble plea. Look - this is from the bottom of my heart, so please read this message to the end. My team and I put together a historic win in 2021 to help flip all three of Virginia's highest offices red. Ever since becoming Lt. Governor, I've been logging big wins for my friends, neighbors, and every fellow Virginian. Now, I'm running for Governor so we can continue to keep up the amazing work we've been doing here in the Commonwealth and make history again. But right now I have a few SERIOUS challenges: I was legally barred from accepting even a dime in support of my campaign while I was in the Virginia legislative session. But my Democrat opponent, Abigail Spanberger, was fundraising BIG TIME while I was working hard in our capital. Seriously , a far-Left group injected $500K into Abigail Spanberger 's campaign coffers and there wasn't a thing I could do in response! Now Spanberger has an ENORMOUS fundraising advantage, Fellow American. Polls show this race is tied, but Spanberger has a serious cash advantage.If we don 't make up the difference, our conservative mission will be cut short. Today, I 'm making a plea to you as a Christian, a mother, and a Marine; please send in any amount you can spare to support my campaign! | | Without your support, we will fall even further behind. The Democrat elitists will log a win and our Commonwealth will regress down a path to irreversible socialism!I wouldn't ask you to stay in this fight if I didn't think it was worth it. And Fellow American, I know we will win if we hold strong and fight this battle together. I am asking you from the bottom of my heart; please stick with me and help us keep the conservative movement thriving! Ever Forward,Lt. Governor Winsome Earle-Sears Immigrant Marine First black female Lt. Governor of VA | Immigrant. Marine. Mom. Small Business Owner. President Trump is on the move again, greeted by an 8 fighter jet escort as Air Force One entered Qatar. His meeting with Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman was very fruitful as the countries agreed to enter a new golden era between the United States and Saudi Arabia. The $600 Billion investment includes energy security, defense industry, technology leadership, and access to global infrastructure and critical minerals. President Trump spoke poignantly about former U.S. leaders using America's military might to dispense justice upon foreign leaders. Making the clear distinction that his role as president to defend America, while it's God's job to sit in judgement. Upon landing in Qatar, the president signed an agreement to generate an economic exchange worth at least $1.2 trillion. President Trump also announced economic deals totaling more than $243.5 billion between the United States and Qatar, including an historic sale of Boeing aircraft and GE Aerospace engines to Qatar Airways. American economist Steve Moore is with me today to unpack the recent investments in the U.S. economy and President Trump's overall economic plan. Moore wants the "big, beautiful bill" passed asap to prevent a huge tax hike and add jet fuel to the economy.

**Steve Moore**
Principal | Committee to Unleash Prosperity
Former Wall Street Journal Editorial Board
American Economist

body:

#
Hi there, I wanted to personally reach out and ask if you could make an urgent [_**$3 donation**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107704548/545554450/701031030?refcode=em-5.14.25-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjIvMS8xMTc1MjUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNWU1MTM3Y2UtMGUzMS1mMDExLThiM2QtNjA0NWJkZWQ4Y2NhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=8pzdaknJ3deBqZ-vXFY89SaWbgAL_Qk9Z__z2jioDNU=) today. Here's why: Tomorrow marks the halfway point through our first fundraising quarter of my campaign for Congress. When this deadline hits, we will have to submit our first report to the FEC. **This will be the first major test of our campaign, and everyone – including the press, the pundits, our opponents – will comb through our report to see if we have what it takes to win this race.** [**Unfortunately, we’re falling a bit short of our goal, and****$3 from you will help get us back on track. Will you chip in now?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107704549/545554451/701031030?refcode=em-5.14.25-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjIvMS8xMTc1MjUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNWU1MTM3Y2UtMGUzMS1mMDExLThiM2QtNjA0NWJkZWQ4Y2NhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=8pzdaknJ3deBqZ-vXFY89SaWbgAL_Qk9Z__z2jioDNU=) | | [DONATE $3](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107704550/545554452/701031030?refcode=em-5.14.25-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjIvMS8xMTc1MjUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNWU1MTM3Y2UtMGUzMS1mMDExLThiM2QtNjA0NWJkZWQ4Y2NhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=8pzdaknJ3deBqZ-vXFY89SaWbgAL_Qk9Z__z2jioDNU=)

Building grassroots strength now is critical to proving that we have the resources to go the distance – especially in a race as expensive as this one.
In case you missed it, a GOP dark money group just started airing ads propping up my opponent on TV – over a year out from Election Day – and you can bet more are on the way.
They’re trying to define this race early, flood the airwaves, and drown out our message before we’ve even had a chance to make our case.
That’s why your support is so important today:
[**Please make a $3 contribution to my campaign today. If we want to take back the House, we must flip key toss-up districts like CA-22.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107704551/545554453/701031030?refcode=em-5.14.25-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjIvMS8xMTc1MjUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNWU1MTM3Y2UtMGUzMS1mMDExLThiM2QtNjA0NWJkZWQ4Y2NhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=8pzdaknJ3deBqZ-vXFY89SaWbgAL_Qk9Z__z2jioDNU=)

clean_body: | Hi there, I wanted to personally reach out and ask if you could make an urgent = today. Here's why: Tomorrow marks the halfway point through our first fundraising quarter of my campaign for Congress. When this deadline hits, we will have to submit our first report to the FEC. This will be the first major test of our campaign, and everyone – including the press, the pundits, our opponents – will comb through our report to see if we have what it takes to win this race. = | | = Building grassroots strength now is critical to proving that we have the resources to go the distance – especially in a race as expensive as this one. In case you missed it, a GOP dark money group just started airing ads propping up my opponent on TV – over a year out from Election Day – and you can bet more are on the way. They’re trying to define this race early, flood the airwaves, and drown out our message before we’ve even had a chance to make our case. name: The Squad
subject: Deadline
body:
[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/18910?refcode=em20250514-1354-foote&t=1001&akid=1354%2E342411%2Eju6U8E ]Squad Victory Fund

Hi Peter,

May is nearly halfway over, and we’ve set a $45,000 mid-month fundraising
goal to ensure our Squad has the resources we need to keep up the fight
for the working class.

We believe in being transparent, so here’s exactly where our fundraising
stands now:

[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/19213?c0=svfem-20250514-movement.4rK5Ox&refcode=em20250514-1354&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1002&akid=1354%2E342411%2Eju6U8E ]May 1, 2025 - May 14, 2025. Total dollars raised: $40,008.59

Here’s the challenge: We are still $4,991.41 shy of our goal – and the
corporate PACs and right-wing billionaires we’re up against would love to
see us fall short.

But our Squad has always been powered by grassroots supporters like you.
Our average donation this month is just over $17, proving that small
contributions from everyday people have the power to fuel our fight.

[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/19651?c0=svfem-20250514-movement.HB8MPQ&refcode=em20250514-1354&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1003&akid=1354%2E342411%2Eju6U8E ]So Peter,
can you pitch in $17 – or whatever makes sense for you – to help us hit
our $45,000 mid-month fundraising goal and keep fighting boldly in
Congress?

[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/19213?c0=svfem-20250514-movement.4rK5Ox&refcode=em20250514-1354&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1022&akid=1354%2E342411%2Eju6U8E ]Contribute ››

In solidarity,

– The Squad

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe:

You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:
http://act.squadvictoryfund.com/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=1354%2E342411%2Eju6U8E

Paid for by The Squad Victory Fund, a joint fundraising committee authorized by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, Rashida Tlaib for Congress, Courage to Change, Inspiring Leadership Has a Name PAC, Power of Us PAC, and Rooted in Community Leadership PAC.

In addition to what’s legally required, The Squad Victory Fund does not accept contributions from: (1) corporations, associations, or their PACs; (2) individuals registered as federal lobbyists or under FARA; (3) from the PACs, lobbyists, or SEC-named executives of fossil fuel companies; or (4) from the Fraternal Order of Police.

Contributions or gifts to The Squad Victory Fund are not tax deductible.

Contributions to The Squad Victory Fund will be allocated as follows: 8.33% to each of Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, Rashida Tlaib for Congress, Courage to Change, Inspiring Leadership Has a Name PAC, Power of Us PAC, Rooted in Community Leadership PAC, Politivist PAC, and For the Children PAC.

Any contributor may designate their contribution for a particular participant. The allocation formula above may change if any contributor makes a contribution that, when allocated, would exceed the amount that the contributor may lawfully give to any participant.

After contributing to The Squad Victory Fund, you will automatically be signed up to receive email updates from Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, and Rashida Tlaib for Congress. You may of course opt-out by unsubscribing at any time.

To contribute via check, please address to The Squad Victory Fund c/o MBA Consulting Group, PO Box 15845, Washington D.C., 20003

Email us: info@squadvictoryfund.com

name: Team Jayapal
subject: 20,000+ strong and growing 💪🏽
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Pramila for Congress
body:
Resistance Lab is training an army of grassroots organizers. Here’s how you can help keep it going.
# [](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/892?refcode=em20250514-5042-footer&t=1&akid=5042%2E420207%2Eljja3D)
We're building something big, Peter. And it's working.
**More than 20,000 people — from more than 43 states _and_ Puerto Rico — have signed up to participate in the Resistance Lab so far:**
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pj-email-20250514-1?refcode=em20250514-5042&amount=3&refcode2=5042_420207_ljja3D&akid=5042%2E420207%2Eljja3D)
That’s 20,000+ grassroots organizers getting trained, fired up, and ready to resist the authoritarian threat Donald Trump and his billionaire backers pose to our country.

clean_body: | Team, Jason is not afraid to take on tough fights. In 2018, he defied the odds by beating a Republican Member of Congress and becoming the first-ever Democrat to hold the seat–the first Democrat to do so in decades. During Donald Trump’s first term, he worked to hold the President accountable by serving as an impeachment manager, where Jason put service to his country above partisan politics. Now, Jason is once again working to fight corruption and stand up for hardworking Colorado families. These fights aren’t easy — especially up against an extremist Republican Party that is beholden to Donald Trump — but that’s not stopping Jason. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | = = | = = | = = A lot is on the line right now. Whether it’s lowering costs for working families, fighting corruption, protecting health care access, or preventing gun violence, Jason is fighting every day to deliver common-sense solutions for all Coloradans. He embodies service above self — putting the needs of his district first. Meanwhile, Republicans are pandering to Donald Trump and Elon Musk, making life worse for families to pass tax cuts for the ultra-wealthy. We have a lot of difficult battles ahead, which is why we need to have Jason’s back and make a strong grassroots showing for his upcoming deadline. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | = = | = = | = = Thank you for being on this team. — Team Crow Jason Crow for Congress 8547 E Arapahoe Road, STE J-543 Greenwood Village, CO 80112 United States | mailto:info@jasoncrowforcongress.com | Paid for by Jason Crow for Congress Jason Crow was a Captain in the U.S. Army. name: Team Bonta
subject: Another reckless misuse of authority
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ROB BONTA FOR CALIFORNIA ATTORNEY GENERAL 2026 FPPC ID #145642
body:
The AG is co-leading a lawsuit against the President’s misuse of emergency powers to fast-track more oil and gas drilling.
# [](https://act.robbonta.com/go/3?refcode=em20250514&t=1&akid=1787%2E1088799%2EsIXvFe)
When Donald Trump signed an unlawful executive order declaring a “national energy emergency” to fast-track new fossil fuel projects at the risk of our health and environment, California’s Attorney General took action.
**Rob is co-leading a coalition of attorneys general who filed a lawsuit to challenge this illegal order in court and push back against this dangerous executive overreach.**
[****](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-20250514?refcode=em20250514-1787&amount=5&refcode2=1787_1088799_sIXvFe&akid=1787%2E1088799%2EsIXvFe)
The facts don’t lie: the United States is producing more energy than ever. Despite this, **Trump is trying to bypass important environmental and health guardrails to expedite permits for non-emergency fossil fuel projects,** while excluding solar and wind power.
Emergency powers are meant for _real_ disasters – not for frivolous or partisan matters. Congress even passed the National Emergencies Act to stop presidents from doing exactly this, but as usual, Trump thinks he is above the law.
**This isn’t about solving a problem or addressing a real emergency. It’s about the President skirting the rules that protect our air, water, and communities, all for the benefit of his Big Oil and Gas buddies.**
We won’t let Trump undo years of progress made on protecting our environment and addressing the climate crisis by breaking the law and misusing his power — not on Rob’s watch.
**[If you believe in protecting California’s air, water, and future, chip in $5 to fuel our movement. With this president breaking the law over and over again, we can’t afford to let up now.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-20250514?refcode=em20250514-1787&amount=5&refcode2=1787_1088799_sIXvFe&akid=1787%2E1088799%2EsIXvFe)**
**[Donate $5 »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-20250514?refcode=em20250514-1787&amount=5&refcode2=1787_1088799_sIXvFe&akid=1787%2E1088799%2EsIXvFe)**
Thanks for standing with Rob,
Team Bonta
* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-wrapper?refcode=em20250514&t=3&refcode2=1787_1088799_sIXvFe&akid=1787%2E1088799%2EsIXvFe)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.robbonta.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=4&akid=1787%2E1088799%2EsIXvFe)

Rob Bonta for Attorney General 2026

name: Senator Dick Anderson
subject: A lot to recap- Unanimous support and more!
body:
Senator Anderson Legislative Newsletter
---
| | | [](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fcontent.govdelivery.com%2Faccounts%2FORLEG%2Fbulletins%2F3df02aa/1/01010196d0e05252-721ac19a-781f-4126-aef3-c9909656062e-000000/28Rikht2ygCXc8EM9_z20Pt12S8Q_0thTEKSTkrYEQQ=405 "View Online")
---|---
---
|
* * *
---
**Landing the plane:**
The long session is moving to a critical time in Salem. The final revenue forecast is out; policy committees are coming to an end and nerves around bills are running high. It is the most interesting part of session for sure, but I think it should not be so decisive.
We have many priorities in Oregon, I get it. However, we have few emergencies that we should be laser focused on. It appears to me, as it probably does to you, all things around housing and cost of living are priorities. If the legislature is not focused on those two areas, it is more than likely just noise and personal priorities.
You will read below the bills my office is working on to help address the emergencies. We have been working, this session on those as priorities. Now, of course I had some other bills, but we chose to let those go and focus on the things that matter most to Senate District 5 and Oregon.
As we move towards the waning days of May, this is the time leadership needs to get involved and make sure we can “land the plane” on the session. Budget, the transportation package and good bills solving emergencies. I hope Leadership does not push tribalism bills around fake gun control priorities, bad rent control policies, and one-sided health care bills not solving problems. I like the leadership we have on both the Republican and Democrat side in the Senate.
I call on them to work together, find common ground, focus on emergencies, and make sure all Oregonians have a voice through their Senators.
Be well,
Dick
* * *
---
* * *
---
I travelled to Klamath Falls for the last Joint Ways and Means Community meeting.
* * *
---
I carried SCR 31 on the Senate floor in Celebration of 50 years of Legislative Commission on Indian Services. I was recently appointed to this committee as well.
* * *
---
The Farm Bureau stopped by my office to discuss their legislative priorities.
* * *
---
Ron and Rosey from Coos Bay Chamber of Commerce stopped in my office while they were in town for Chamber Lobby Day.
* * *
---
Building Resilience stopped by to talk about their legislative priorities for the session.
* * *
---
* * *
---
There are many bills that I am a chief sponsor of that are still in the running to become laws at the end of session in June. Eleven of these bills can be broken down into four different sections: Housing, Utilities, News and Education.
In education there are two bills up for consideration- [SB 896](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Folis.oregonlegislature.gov%2Fliz%2F2025R1%2FMeasures%2FOverview%2FSB896/1/01010196d0e05252-721ac19a-781f-4126-aef3-c9909656062e-000000/xHg-E06QxNnc5xnLCt4pPZ23YlrcHrmdVeaNHBFOcU8=405) and [SB 1099](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Folis.oregonlegislature.gov%2Fliz%2F2025R1%2FMeasures%2FOverview%2FSB1099/1/01010196d0e05252-721ac19a-781f-4126-aef3-c9909656062e-000000/QGFR8C09yDLlE6-GoT_zMrqvBFA5GVkIox6FRNCVn2Y=405). Both bills are much needed pieces of legislation to help children in our state. Oregon ranks poorly compared to other states in education and we are falling further and further behind. We need to help them. Systemic issues need to be addressed and corrected, and this legislation helps with that.
[Senate Bill 896](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Folis.oregonlegislature.gov%2Fliz%2F2025R1%2FMeasures%2FOverview%2FSB896/2/01010196d0e05252-721ac19a-781f-4126-aef3-c9909656062e-000000/kEM9Wt_Iieg4ujKjCYlve1AV2JHLcuPYirgl16Qzsxc=405) creates a grant program to be used to fund after-school learning and activities. Investing in our children in this area will yield long-term benefits such as academic support, social and emotional growth, reducing risky behaviors in hours unsupervised, parent support and future successes. Studies consistently show that unsupervised children after school face higher rates of delinquency, with violent juvenile crime peaking from the hours of 3:00-6:00 PM. Approximately twenty percent of U.S. children are unsupervised weekly. This correlates with increased absenteeism, substance use and behavioral issues. Funding after school programs can help mitigate these risks. Supervised children show better outcomes and improved academics. This is needed for the children of Oregon to succeed. This bill was referred to Ways and Means and should be on the schedule soon. I will keep you updated.
Another bill that is desperately needed for children in Oregon is [Senate Bill 1099](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Folis.oregonlegislature.gov%2Fliz%2F2025R1%2FMeasures%2FOverview%2FSB1099/2/01010196d0e05252-721ac19a-781f-4126-aef3-c9909656062e-000000/nk4_hlStXLy4Z_M85G2BRNZYddvEDbLUq3Us-SP9DWI=405). This bill allows for cities and counties to allow preschools on church properties. Oregon faces consequential childcare shortages, with most counties classified as childcare deserts. This means there are 3 children for every regulated childcare spot available. Across Oregon, only 12 percent of infants and toddlers and 29 percent of pre-school-age children have access to regulated childcare slots. One of the issues faced with childcare is cost-effective facilities. Churches often have underutilized spaces during weekdays, which can transition to preschools at low or no rent. This reduces the startup and operational costs compared to leasing commercial spaces making childcare more affordable. If a childcare facility is built using federal, state, or local government funds, prevailing wage laws apply to the project. This means that the initial cost of the project can jump anywhere from 10-30 percent, compliance of this can be extremely burdensome for smaller childcare facilities and nonprofits resulting in childcare facilities not being built at all. Church-based preschools in Oregon can address the childcare deserts by leveraging low-costs, trusted and accessible facilities. This bill passed the Senate with unanimous consent and was sent to the house where it had a work session in the House Early Childhood and Human Services committee. From there it will head to the house floor to be voted on to become a law.
Three bills that I am a chief sponsor for relate to newspapers and media. These are [Senate Bills 433](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Folis.oregonlegislature.gov%2Fliz%2F2025R1%2FMeasures%2FOverview%2FSB433/1/01010196d0e05252-721ac19a-781f-4126-aef3-c9909656062e-000000/fXrIORzlaGuCsXd6K4C9crvMPfYrRQ3qA3r2F-UMPNk=405), [437](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Folis.oregonlegislature.gov%2Fliz%2F2025R1%2FMeasures%2FOverview%2FSB437/1/01010196d0e05252-721ac19a-781f-4126-aef3-c9909656062e-000000/KrVnoYAH4XEXF-qao0MufbHbb2Ruw-_YbpEEIao73cw=405) and [686](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Folis.oregonlegislature.gov%2Fliz%2F2025R1%2FMeasures%2FOverview%2FSB686/1/01010196d0e05252-721ac19a-781f-4126-aef3-c9909656062e-000000/2EFfbfqrWz9SDHiOZw-TJ7hBG0zLXtdQuWgDMNLSrtI=405). All these bills highlight a need for change in how media is distributed in Oregon and the move to digital alternatives.
[Senate Bill 433 A](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Folis.oregonlegislature.gov%2Fliz%2F2025R1%2FMeasures%2FOverview%2FSB433/2/01010196d0e05252-721ac19a-781f-4126-aef3-c9909656062e-000000/S0ZZIju2K861mWfKOverrooHL16hlg1dfCNXidNjMnU=405) is needed to modernize and streamline the process for self-service storage facility owners to handle personal property subject to liens for unpaid rent. By raising the disposal threshold from $300 to $1000 and allowing online sales for higher-value items, this bill reduces financial and administrative burdens on owners, aligns the law with current economic realities and supports small businesses. At the same time, it maintains protections for tenants, ensuring a balanced approach. This targeted update addresses practical challenges in the storage industry while promoting efficiency and fairness. This bill was most recently heard in the House committee on Commerce and Consumer Protection, once it is voted on it will be sent to the House for a vote on the floor.
[Senate Bill 437](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Folis.oregonlegislature.gov%2Fliz%2F2025R1%2FMeasures%2FOverview%2FSB437/2/01010196d0e05252-721ac19a-781f-4126-aef3-c9909656062e-000000/KOibnDlUm5697y6U5-Vwvg0sQvlvA0C2sMSdfXi8THc=405) goes in tandem with SB 433, by amending various statues related to public notice requirements, modernizing the way notices are published by expanding the definition of acceptable publication platforms to include digital “news publications” alongside traditional newspapers. Existing Oregon laws require many public notices be published in a “newspaper” meeting specific criterion, such as print format, circulation, and subscriber requirements. These rules were designed for a print-dominated era and do not account for the shift to digital media, where many people now access their news online. With the decline of newspapers a lot of Oregon counties, particularly those in rural areas, have seen no qualifying newspapers exist and forcing reliance on distant publications with limited local reach. By broadening the definition to include digital news publications and allowing publications in other nearby published outlets, SB 437 ensures that public notice can still be disseminated effectively, even in areas with limited print media. This bill is still in the Senate committee on Rules and will hopefully move to be voted on the Senate floor soon.
The last bill relating to news and media is [Senate Bill 686.](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Folis.oregonlegislature.gov%2Fliz%2F2025R1%2FMeasures%2FOverview%2FSB686/2/01010196d0e05252-721ac19a-781f-4126-aef3-c9909656062e-000000/5wUXus9yIQunzfzwq5emTkc2syOl-gBX9B0sFB-7K80=405) This bill aims to address challenges facing local journalism and civic information access by requiring large online platforms (e.g. Google and Meta) to financially support digital journalism providers. This bill is a response to the decline of local news and the growing influence of digital platforms. SB 686 requires large online platforms, those with over 50 million monthly U.S. users or significant market capitalization) to compensate digital journalism providers or donate to a civic information consortium. Online platforms display and distribute news content without directly compensating the journalism providers who create it. This allows platforms to profit from journalistic work while news organizations struggle, as platforms capture most of the digital advertising revenue. This bill supports jobs for journalists and support staff, boosting local economies, particularly in areas with struggling news outlets. SB 686 could potentially serve as a model for other states, by positioning Oregon as a leader in addressing the journalism crisis. This bill most recently had a work session in the Senate Committee on Rules where it passed, this bill will be headed to the Senate floor to be voted on next.
Shifting gears to utilities and housing there are a few bills that are still in the running to become laws. [Senate Bill 1062](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Folis.oregonlegislature.gov%2Fliz%2F2025R1%2FMeasures%2FOverview%2FSB1062/1/01010196d0e05252-721ac19a-781f-4126-aef3-c9909656062e-000000/XJDArJ0kFqBLNz485J2Y0pdrIvzbTizR2FH2TtoaJig=405) focuses on three specific cities- North Bend, Reedsport, and Bandon. SB 1062 grants these cities greater local control by aligning their fee-setting process with standard democratic practice (majority vote), which still ensures accountability. This balance also allows the cities to act decisively while remaining answerable to residents through regular elections and public meetings. Aging water and wastewater systems in small cities require significant investment This bill passed the senate 25-4 and most recently had its public hearing in the House committee on Emergency Management, General Government and Health.
Moving to housing [Senate Bill 684 A](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Folis.oregonlegislature.gov%2Fliz%2F2025R1%2FMeasures%2FOverview%2FSB684/1/01010196d0e05252-721ac19a-781f-4126-aef3-c9909656062e-000000/C32j6l3rXMLfmTHpeoD3pHXgPLFPcYozF9nqwjVxc3Y=405) is making its way through the Senate where it is currently in the Senate Committee on Finance and Revenue and then will make its way to the Joint Ways and Means Committee. SB 684 (A-engrossed) is an important piece of legislation that tackles Oregon’s affordable housing crisis by promoting mixed-income housing through innovative financing, expanding housing powers, and long-term affordability protections. By establishing a loan program fund, removing regulatory barriers, and ensuring tax exemptions, it allows the increase supply of affordable housing, and fostering economic integration. Its focus on sustainability, transparency and flexibility makes it a critical step towards meeting Oregon’s housing needs, particularly for lower-and moderate-income households. This bill is also having bipartisan support and is aligned with the states housing goals while it emphasizes a significance in forward-thinking policy solution.
Another bill that is significant and has bipartisan support is [Senate Bill 1086](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Folis.oregonlegislature.gov%2Fliz%2F2025R1%2FMeasures%2FOverview%2FSB1086/1/01010196d0e05252-721ac19a-781f-4126-aef3-c9909656062e-000000/YzMvJXAadCFB_ScrsGPll0jBD1kfN2_PCPbfnS9Jwlc=405) (A-Engrossed). This bill addresses Oregon’s shortage of building inspectors, improve competence and uniform enforcement of building codes, and support the state’s housing and construction goals. By directing DCBS to assist OBOA in developing an apprenticeship program and formalizing apprenticeships as a certification pathway, the bill creates a practical, assessable solution to workforce challenges. This bill is a collaborative approach with a temporary mandate that aligns with state standards to ensure a targeted and effective response to a critical issue in the state. This bill’s implications extend to workforce development, public safety and housing production, making SB 1086 a key component in Oregon’s 2025 legislative session.
[Senate Bill 974](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Folis.oregonlegislature.gov%2Fliz%2F2025R1%2FMeasures%2FOverview%2FSB974/1/01010196d0e05252-721ac19a-781f-4126-aef3-c9909656062e-000000/iNQYcxM3_EKAECasanlSnCgLFIt1De0VtVF02DPuNX4=405) (A-Engrossed) aims to accelerate the review process for housing permits by setting strict timelines for local governments and special districts to process final engineering plans and urban housing applications, while also introducing penalties for delays and waiving certain design review requirements. One of the many things that is exacerbating the housing crisis in Oregon is the lengthy permitting and review process. Local governments often take months to review plans and land use applications, delaying construction and increasing costs for developers. These delays hinder the states’ ability to meet housing production goals. These delays also increase the cost of a house for a consumer because a prolonged review process increases a developer’s carrying costs, which are passed on to the homebuyers. Senate Bill 974 will require local governments and special districts to confirm within 14 days whether final engineering plans for residential development within an Urban Growth Boundary are completed or specify additional materials needed. It will also require a complete review be ready to issue site development permits within 90 days of a complete application. A streamlined process will accelerate construction, create jobs and stimulate local economies. Reduced delays also attract developers to Oregon, boosting investment in housing and infrastructure. This bill passed unanimously in the Senate and is now working its way through the house with public hearings and a work session scheduled.

clean_body: Senator Anderson Legislative Newsletter | | | "View Online" ---|--- | Landing the plane: The long session is moving to a critical time in Salem. The final revenue forecast is out; policy committees are coming to an end and nerves around bills are running high. It is the most interesting part of session for sure, but I think it should not be so decisive. We have many priorities in Oregon, I get it. However, we have few emergencies that we should be laser focused on. It appears to me, as it probably does to you, all things around housing and cost of living are priorities. If the legislature is not focused on those two areas, it is more than likely just noise and personal priorities. You will read below the bills my office is working on to help address the emergencies. We have been working, this session on those as priorities. Now, of course I had some other bills, but we chose to let those go and focus on the things that matter most to Senate District 5 and Oregon. As we move towards the waning days of May, this is the time leadership needs to get involved and make sure we can “land the plane” on the session. Budget, the transportation package and good bills solving emergencies. I hope Leadership does not push tribalism bills around fake gun control priorities, bad rent control policies, and one-sided health care bills not solving problems. I like the leadership we have on both the Republican and Democrat side in the Senate. I call on them to work together, find common ground, focus on emergencies, and make sure all Oregonians have a voice through their Senators. Be well, Dick I travelled to Klamath Falls for the last Joint Ways and Means Community meeting. I carried SCR 31 on the Senate floor in Celebration of 50 years of Legislative Commission on Indian Services. I was recently appointed to this committee as well. The Farm Bureau stopped by my office to discuss their legislative priorities. Ron and Rosey from Coos Bay Chamber of Commerce stopped in my office while they were in town for Chamber Lobby Day. Building Resilience stopped by to talk about their legislative priorities for the session. There are many bills that I am a chief sponsor of that are still in the running to become laws at the end of session in June. Eleven of these bills can be broken down into four different sections: Housing, Utilities, News and Education. In education there are two bills up for consideration- and . Both bills are much needed pieces of legislation to help children in our state. Oregon ranks poorly compared to other states in education and we are falling further and further behind. We need to help them. Systemic issues need to be addressed and corrected, and this legislation helps with that. creates a grant program to be used to fund after-school learning and activities. Investing in our children in this area will yield long-term benefits such as academic support, social and emotional growth, reducing risky behaviors in hours unsupervised, parent support and future successes. Studies consistently show that unsupervised children after school face higher rates of delinquency, with violent juvenile crime peaking from the hours of 3:00-6:00 PM. Approximately twenty percent of U.S. children are unsupervised weekly. This correlates with increased absenteeism, substance use and behavioral issues. Funding after school programs can help mitigate these risks. Supervised children show better outcomes and improved academics. This is needed for the children of Oregon to succeed. This bill was referred to Ways and Means and should be on the schedule soon. I will keep you updated. Another bill that is desperately needed for children in Oregon is . This bill allows for cities and counties to allow preschools on church properties. Oregon faces consequential childcare shortages, with most counties classified as childcare deserts. This means there are 3 children for every regulated childcare spot available. Across Oregon, only 12 percent of infants and toddlers and 29 percent of pre-school-age children have access to regulated childcare slots. One of the issues faced with childcare is cost-effective facilities. Churches often have underutilized spaces during weekdays, which can transition to preschools at low or no rent. This reduces the startup and operational costs compared to leasing commercial spaces making childcare more affordable. If a childcare facility is built using federal, state, or local government funds, prevailing wage laws apply to the project. This means that the initial cost of the project can jump anywhere from 10-30 percent, compliance of this can be extremely burdensome for smaller childcare facilities and nonprofits resulting in childcare facilities not being built at all. Church-based preschools in Oregon can address the childcare deserts by leveraging low-costs, trusted and accessible facilities. This bill passed the Senate with unanimous consent and was sent to the house where it had a work session in the House Early Childhood and Human Services committee. From there it will head to the house floor to be voted on to become a law. Three bills that I am a chief sponsor for relate to newspapers and media. These are , and . All these bills highlight a need for change in how media is distributed in Oregon and the move to digital alternatives. is needed to modernize and streamline the process for self-service storage facility owners to handle personal property subject to liens for unpaid rent. By raising the disposal threshold from $300 to $1000 and allowing online sales for higher-value items, this bill reduces financial and administrative burdens on owners, aligns the law with current economic realities and supports small businesses. At the same time, it maintains protections for tenants, ensuring a balanced approach. This targeted update addresses practical challenges in the storage industry while promoting efficiency and fairness.
